concerns.text: Phase 6 (T028-T032) introduces 'Scaling Law & Cost Analysis' and 'cost-metabolism' metrics. These deliverables have no corresponding Functional Requirement (FR) or User Story in spec.md. This violates the constraint that tasks must not silently relax or expand scope beyond the spec; adding unapproved features is a scope violation.

T017 implements 'Leave-One-Out PCA (LOO-PCA)' excluding Python. While the plan.md notes this as a methodological improvement, spec.md FR-005 explicitly mandates 'PCA on all 12 languages'. The task delivers a different algorithm than the spec requires without an explicit 'staged' or 'amendment' flag in the task description, constituting a semantic weakening of the FR.

T018 implements a 'Generalized Linear Mixed Model (GLMM)'. Spec.md FR-005 mandates a 'Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM)'. While GLMM is statistically superior for binary data, the task delivers a different model class than the spec demands. Without an explicit note in the task that this is a 'Constitution VI override' or 'Spec Amendment', this is a constraint violation.

T021 implements 'secondary non-parametric tests (Wilcoxon signed-rank)'. Spec.md FR-005 does not explicitly list these tests, though Constitution Principle VI requires them. The task covers a constitutional requirement not explicitly codified in the FR it claims to satisfy (US2/FR-005). This creates a traceability gap where the task satisfies a higher-order constraint not visible in the immediate FR, risking silent drift if the FR is updated.

T025 describes a 'contamination filter' comparing dates. Spec.md FR-006 and Constitution Principle VII define the logic as 'Task Release Date < Model Training Cutoff'. The task description is vague ('compares task release dates to model training cut-offs') and does not explicitly mandate the strict inequality logic required to prevent data leakage, risking a semantic relaxation of the contamination control constraint.
